subject: AI for the factory floor — 0 to $6.1M in 20 months
body:
Hey Greg,

I came across your piece on Redefining Physical Industries and one line stuck with me: "We are seeing a perfect storm that I personally and the team at Eclipse believes will" I think that's exactly right. And I think the practical reality of making it happen is harder than most people in venture appreciate.

I'd love to talk. Here's a link to book 30: [calendly]

I co-founded CloudNC ten years ago. We spent seven years solving the CAM programming automation problem that nobody else has managed to crack. In the last 20 months we went from zero to $6.1M in ARR on $6.2M in GTM spend. Four salespeople. Two of thirty CAM packages integrated. Every incumbent except Dassault has partnered with us. Raising $15M. Agentic CAM is next and we're the only ones positioned to deliver it.

The TAM is roughly $300B of cognitive labor in precision machining worldwide. We're building the AI orchestration layer for factories.

subject: Recommended to you — CAM automation, $6.1M ARR
body:
Hey Matt,

I've been recommended to you from a few different directions and I'm reading up on your background. I love your thesis — I think it's being proved right now. I'm impressed by the bets you've made around physical industries where a lot of people talk about this but you've been doing it since before it was cool. Frankly Paperless, Fictiv — I actually know Jason at Paperless pretty well.

I'd love to talk. Here's a link to book 30: [calendly]

I co-founded CloudNC ten years ago to automate CAM programming, which I'm sure you know a lot about and no one has managed to solve yet. We figured it out and in the last 20 months we went from zero to $6.1M in ARR on $6.2M in GTM spend. The market is desperate for a product and we're scaling into it rapidly. Raising $15M to take this to $100M now that it works, and then on to a generational company from there. Automating CAM is the single largest AI opportunity in the manufacturing industry.

That was with just two of thirty CAM packages that matter and with a non-agentic solution that is currently becoming agentic. Every incumbent in the industry except for Dassault has partnered with us, having tried and failed.

subject: Carl recommended I reach out — manufacturing AI
body:
Hey Josh, hope you don't mind me reaching out directly. You've been recommended to me from a number of directions, including Carl. I love your investing history. You were investing in atoms before atoms became the big thing recently. I felt for a long time like software engineering was going to go to zero and then the bottleneck for capturing value would move to the next layer and that's exactly what's happening right now.

What I think is missing in the reindustrialization thesis is that no war was ever won by building new factories. It was all won by converting existing factories to a war effort. Right now the US has one machinist for every two it needs and that's about to become 4x by 2035. I think the country that wins in this next industrial revolution is going to be the one that manages to nail AI orchestration of factories, multiplying productivity by more than 4x in their existing industrial base.

I know I'm basically riffing off Anduril here but that's what I believe. I'd love a chance to talk about this. Here's my meeting link: [calendly]

Co-founded CloudNC ten years ago. Spent a decade solving the CAM automation problem, which now works. In the last 20 months we scaled from zero to $6.1M ARR with $6.2M of GTM spend, nearly a thousand factories globally, and that was with just four salespeople and two integrations. We're about to have dozens of integrations and agentic CAM. My intention is to build the AI orchestration layer for existing installed factory bases. Would love to talk.

subject: Picking up the conversation — CloudNC's pivot worked
body:
Hey Lee, we spoke a few years ago, back when I was still building autonomous factories directly. A couple of years ago our tech hit the point where we could sell it to any factory, regardless of their machinery, tools, and parts. Since then we've killed the factory business and we've been scaling that up.

I remember your conviction around the physical world. I loved the welding business that you invested in and I'd love to pick up the conversation again. Here's my booking link: [calendly]

What's changed: pivoted to software only now that we're capable. Acquired $6.1M in ARR with $6.2M in GTM spend in 20 months, with only four salespeople and two integrations of thirty that matter. Almost every incumbent in the industry has now partnered with us to take the rest of the industry. We have an agentic solution coming soon and now that the tech is working we get to automate the entire factory layer. Raising $15M to take this to $100M in ARR and $50M in 12 months to take our solution global and build a generational company. Let's talk.

I think the reindustrialization thesis is right but most people are missing the bottleneck. It's not factories — it's the cognitive layer inside factories. The US has one machinist for every two it needs. That ratio is getting worse. The country that solves this wins.
